title: Garlicky Flaky Algerian Lamb Tagine
description: A flavorful and aromatic tagine dish that combines the richness of lamb with the brightness of garlic and the warmth of spices, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This recipe is designed to be easy to make and adaptable to ayurvedic and sattvic diets.

ingredients.name: lamb shoulder, boneless and cut into 1-inch pieces
ingredients.notes: You may use lamb shoulder or lamb neck, whichever is available.

ingredients.name: olive oil
ingredients.notes: Use a good-quality, extra-virgin olive oil for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: onion
ingredients.notes: Chop the onion into small, uniform pieces.

ingredients.name: garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince the garlic and saut with the onion for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: garlic powder
ingredients.notes: Add a pinch of garlic powder to complement the fresh garlic.

ingredients.name: ground ginger
ingredients.notes: Grate the ginger using a microplane for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: ground cinnamon
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality cinnamon for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: ground cumin
ingredients.notes: Add a pinch of cumin to complement the coriander.

ingredients.name: ground coriander
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality coriander for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: black pepper
ingredients.notes: Use freshly ground black pepper for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: chopped fresh parsley
ingredients.notes: Add the parsley as a garnish for a pop of color.

ingredients.name: sazon
ingredients.notes: Add the sazon for a pinch of saltiness and umami flavor.

ingredients.name: lemon juice
ingredients.notes: Add the lemon juice for a burst of citrus flavor.

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large, heavy pot or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until it is softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the ground ginger, ground cinnamon, ground cumin, and ground coriander, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the lamb shoulder and cook, stirring occasionally, until it is browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the water, salt, black pepper, sazon, and lemon juice, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot with a tight-fitting lid, and simmer for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and falls apart easily.
Add chopped fresh parsley as a garnish, if desired.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with parsley and accompanied by couscous or rice.
